legs.leg: Safari circuit - Tarangire / Lake Manyara / Ngorongoro Crater
legs.lodging: Suricata Safaris (Twiga camps + Jevas Hotel, package)

notes.content: Vasilis is planning a fully booked international trip in August 2026, traveling with a companion named Athina Mari. The trip spans Cairo (Egypt), Arusha and the northern Tanzania safari circuit, Zanzibar, and Dar es Salaam, running August 3–20. The trip combines cultural sightseeing in Cairo, a group camping safari through Tarangire, Lake Manyara, and Ngorongoro Crater, beach time in Zanzibar, and city exploration in Dar es Salaam. All flights, accommodations, the safari, and key dining are confirmed and fully organized. Vasilis communicates with Claude in both Greek and English interchangeably; Claude should respond in whichever language Vasilis uses.
All trip logistics are confirmed. No unbooked gaps remain.

Suricata Safaris Ltd, 3-day group camping safari, Aug 7–9. Contact: Richard, +255 763 222 293, info@suricatasafaris.com. Cost: $1,200 total; $240 deposit paid (20%); $960 balance due on arrival in cash (USD, post-2009 notes) or online via Pesapal (+5% surcharge): payments.pesapal.com/suricatasafaris.

The Rock Restaurant, Zanzibar — Aug 15, 18:00–20:00 sitting, 2 adults, 2x Tropical Passion Fruit Cheesecake included. Fully paid ($46). Outdoor seating requested for sunset/star views. Contact: +255 776 591 360 / +255 763 070 658, booking@therockrestaurantzanzibar.com.

Vasilis holds an International Driving Permit (IDP). A local Zanzibar Driving Permit is also required (~$15, valid up to 3 months). Most rental companies arrange this with a license photo provided 24+ hours before arrival. Police checkpoints are frequent; carry both IDP and local permit (screenshot acceptable).

Safari balance ($960 USD, post-2009 notes) to be paid on arrival — cash preferred; Pesapal online adds 5%.
Cairo Stopover 1 sightseeing plan: Grand Egyptian Museum and Pyramids of Giza (Aug 3–4). Eileen Hotel is near Tahrir Square/Nile Corniche.
Cairo Stopover 2 sightseeing plan: Islamic Cairo, the Citadel, Khan el-Khalili bazaar, Coptic Cairo (Aug 19–20). Ramses Hilton is the base; taxi needed between Citadel and Coptic Cairo.
Zanzibar activities and local exploration, including The Rock Restaurant dinner on Aug 15.
Dar es Salaam: arriving by ferry from Zanzibar; interest in city exploration (Kivukoni Fish Market, Kariakoo Market, National Museum, Coco Beach, Bongoyo Island).

EgyptAir operates the Cairo↔Dar es Salaam legs — confirmed by Vasilis; do not second-guess this.
Tanzania e-visa is mandatory online ($50/person) at visa.immigration.go.tz; visa-on-arrival no longer available as of January 2025. Zanzibar also requires separate mandatory travel insurance from the Zanzibar Insurance Corporation.
Egypt e-visa is multiple-entry ($65); both travelers apply separately. Egyptian e-visa requires a mandatory printed copy.
Ngorongoro nights are cold — warm layers needed.
For Zanzibar swimming: north coast (Nungwi area) offers more consistent conditions vs. east/south coasts where low tide limits access.
Precision Air flights (TC144/TC145) have luggage restrictions favoring soft bags.
August is peak safari season — ideal for wildlife, including the Great Wildebeest Migration corridor.
Vasilis prefers simple, concise output formats (bullet lists over tables) unless a table is specifically requested.

Safari operator: Suricata Safaris Ltd — Richard (+255 763 222 293, info@suricatasafaris.com)
Safari payment: Pesapal (payments.pesapal.com/suricatasafaris) — 5% surcharge applies
Eileen Hotel Cairo: +20 15 56434043, info@eileenhotel.com — 21 Youssef El-Gendy St, Abdeen, Downtown Cairo (near Tahrir Square)
Serengeti Villa Arusha: +255 757 757 928
The Rock Restaurant Zanzibar: +255 776 591 360 / +255 763 070 658, booking@therockrestaurantzanzibar.com
Zanzibar Insurance Corporation: for mandatory Zanzibar travel insurance
Tanzania e-visa portal: visa.immigration.go.tz
Egypt e-visa portal: previously used for application
Safari clothing: Decathlon (Forclaz trekking line) recommended as primary source in Greece; DEET-based insect repellent essential.

Driver-guide: Emmanuel (excellent — not Richard from the office).

At the rim: bought 2 beaded bracelets for Athina Mari, ~TZS 35,000 (~$20–25 USD).

Ranger tip: ~TZS 5,000 cash at the gate. This is separate from park fees and was not previously disclosed.

⚠️ Action item: Ask Richard about the ranger tip before paying the safari balance. This may be expected at the crater gate on entry day and should be budgeted separately.

Park fees (from NCAA Tariffs — non-EA adult, Aug 2026 peak season):
• Entry permit: $70.80/person (1 day, single entry within 24 hours)
• Crater service fee: $70.80/person (single entry)
• Vehicle descent fee: $295.00/vehicle/trip (non-EA, tare weight up to 2,000 kg)
• Total park fees for 2 pax: $578.20 (included in safari package via Suricata)

Notes from the tariff PDF: Only 4×4 vehicles up to 3,500 kg tare weight allowed on crater floor. Crater descent gates close at 16:00, close at 18:00. Speed limit 25 kph on crater roads. Max 5 vehicles around a sighting. All crater visitors must be accompanied by a licensed guide or official NCAA guide. Walking safaris require an NCAA armed ranger. Ngorongoro nights are cold — warm layers essential.

Architecture photo description: A bright courtyard scene in Stone Town, Zanzibar. The building is white with dark wooden balconies and arched windows. Warm brown wood contrasts against the white walls, under a clear blue sky — classic Zanzibar architecture.

This captures the distinctive Zanzibari style: coral rag walls finished in white lime wash, ornate dark-wood (typically teak or mahogany) balconies with carved lattice work, and arched/ogee windows with wooden shutters. The combination of bright white, warm wood tones, and the deep tropical blue sky is quintessentially Stone Town.

Driver-guide: Emmanuel (excellent — not Richard from the office).

At the rim: bought 2 beaded bracelets for Athina Mari, ~TZS 35,000 (~$20–25 USD).

Ranger tip: ~TZS 5,000 cash at the gate. This is separate from park fees and was not previously disclosed.

⚠️ Action item: Ask Richard about the ranger tip before paying the safari balance. This may be expected at the crater gate on entry day and should be budgeted separately.

• Vehicle descent fee: $295.00/vehicle/trip (non-EA, tare weight up to 2,000 kg)
• Total park fees for 2 pax: $578.20 (included in safari package via Suricata)

Max 5 vehicles around a sighting. All crater visitors must be accompanied by a licensed guide or official NCAA guide. Walking safaris require an NCAA armed ranger.
